Toyota Auto Detailing Service Prices in Columbia & Lexington SC (2026 Guide)

By Mikah Albertson — updated 2026-03-20

Toyota owners across Columbia and Lexington, SC are some of the most value-conscious vehicle owners on the road — which makes complete sense, because Toyotas are engineered to last. A well-maintained Camry, RAV4, or Tacoma can run for 200,000+ miles, and professional detailing is one of the most important steps you can take to protect that investment. Whether you're driving a daily-commuter Corolla or a weekend-ready 4Runner, keeping the paint, interior, and exterior surfaces properly maintained preserves resale value and protects against South Carolina's intense UV rays, humidity, and pollen.

If you've been searching for Toyota auto detailing service prices in Columbia or Lexington SC, this guide breaks down exactly what you can expect to pay — by model — along with the services that make the biggest difference for Toyota vehicles specifically.

Toyota Auto Detailing Prices by Model (2026)

Pricing is primarily driven by vehicle size and the time required to properly detail each surface. Here is how Mikah's Auto Detailing prices Toyota models across our two service packages:

Toyota Camry & Corolla (Sedans)

The Camry and Corolla are among the most popular vehicles we detail in Columbia SC. Their manageable size and relatively straightforward interior layouts make them efficient to work on without cutting corners.

  • Basic Detail: $225 — includes full hand wash, wheel and tire cleaning, exterior drying and dressing, interior vacuum, dashboard and console wipe-down, window cleaning inside and out, and door jamb cleaning
  • Factory Reset (Full Detail): $400 — everything in the Basic Detail plus clay bar decontamination, paint sealant application, carpet and upholstery extraction, leather conditioning, full trim and plastic restoration, and trunk cleaning

The Camry in particular tends to show swirl marks over time — especially darker colors like Midnight Black Metallic — so a Factory Reset detail with a light polish step is something we frequently recommend for Camry owners.

Toyota RAV4 & Highlander (Mid-Size SUVs)

The RAV4 is Toyota's best-selling model nationally, and it's everywhere in the Columbia and Lexington area. The Highlander adds three rows and significantly more interior surface to clean. Both fall into our mid-size SUV tier.

  • Basic Detail: $225–$250 — same exterior and interior service as our sedan tier, scaled to the larger footprint and additional cargo area
  • Factory Reset (Full Detail): $400–$450 — full decontamination, sealant, full interior extraction including the cargo area, all trim restored

RAV4 Trail and TRD Off-Road owners who take their vehicles off-road will often need additional time for undercarriage and wheel well cleaning. We assess this during booking and adjust accordingly.

Toyota Tacoma (Mid-Size Truck)

The Tacoma is one of the most-detailed trucks we service across both Columbia and Lexington. Tacoma owners tend to use their trucks and want them to look great — which means the detailing work reflects real-world grime from job sites, trails, and hauling.

  • Basic Detail: $225–$250 — full exterior wash, wheels, tires, cab interior vacuum and wipe-down, windows, door jambs; bed liner and bed walls are rinsed and cleaned as part of the standard service
  • Factory Reset (Full Detail): $400–$450 — complete decontamination, paint sealant, full cab interior extraction, leather or cloth conditioning, trim restoration; bed cleaning with degreaser included when needed

Note on the bed: if the Tacoma bed has heavy buildup from mulch, gravel, chemicals, or construction materials, we may add a small surcharge for deep bed cleaning. We always communicate this upfront before starting any work.

Toyota 4Runner & Sequoia (Full-Size SUVs)

The 4Runner is a cult favorite for a reason — it's built like a tank. But its boxy shape, textured plastic cladding, and three-row Sequoia variant mean there is significantly more surface area to properly detail compared to a sedan or crossover.

  • Basic Detail: $250 — full exterior wash and dress, wheels and tires, interior vacuum across all rows, dashboard and console wipe-down, window cleaning, door jambs
  • Factory Reset (Full Detail): $450 — full decontamination, paint sealant, complete multi-row interior extraction, all plastic cladding restored with appropriate dressing, leather or cloth conditioning, cargo area cleaned

The 4Runner's textured plastic body cladding is particularly vulnerable to fading and chalking in the South Carolina sun. A Factory Reset detail includes proper plastic restoration that significantly improves the appearance of faded trim — something that makes a dramatic visual difference on older 4Runners.

Toyota Tundra (Full-Size Truck)

The Tundra is our largest standard Toyota detailing job. Second-generation and third-generation Tundras are big trucks with big cabs and big beds — all of which take more time and product to do properly.

  • Basic Detail: $250 — exterior wash, wheels, tires, cab interior vacuum and wipe-down, windows, door jambs, bed rinse and basic clean
  • Factory Reset (Full Detail): $450+ — full exterior decontamination, paint sealant, complete cab interior extraction including rear seats and floor, trim and plastic restoration, bed deep clean; pricing may adjust upward for CrewMax configurations with maximum interior surface area or for beds with heavy soiling

Tundra owners who use their truck for work — construction, landscaping, hauling — should expect their detail to be on the higher end of the range. We never charge more than quoted without discussing it with you first.

Ceramic Coating for Toyota Vehicles

South Carolina's sun is relentless. UV exposure causes paint oxidation, fading, and clear coat degradation faster here than in most other states. Toyota's factory paint, while solid, is not immune to these conditions — and once clear coat damage sets in, repair costs escalate quickly.

Ceramic coating is the most durable paint protection option available, providing 2–5 years of hydrophobic, UV-resistant protection in a single application. For Toyota owners planning to keep their vehicle for years, ceramic coating is one of the best investments you can make in protecting resale value.

We provide custom ceramic coating quotes based on vehicle condition, paint correction needs, and desired coating tier. Contact us at (803) 667-8731 to discuss your Toyota and get an accurate estimate.

Paint Correction for Toyota Vehicles

Paint correction is a service we frequently recommend for Toyotas that have been regularly washed at automatic car washes. The spinning brushes and abrasive cloth systems in automated washes are the single biggest cause of swirl marks on Toyota paint — particularly on dark-colored vehicles like the Camry XSE in Midnight Black or the Tundra in Magnetic Gray.

Common paint correction needs we see on Toyotas include:

  • Swirl marks from automated car washes or improper hand washing technique
  • Water spot etching from hard water or leaving the vehicle unwashed after rain
  • Oxidation on older vehicles, particularly white or silver paint that has lost its depth
  • Light scratches from parking lot encounters, keys, or brush contact

Paint correction pricing is quoted individually based on the severity of defects, the size of the vehicle, and the number of correction stages needed. Reach out for a free assessment — we can often estimate over photos before you even schedule.

Why Mobile Detailing Makes Sense for Toyota Owners in Columbia & Lexington

One of the most consistent pieces of feedback we hear from Toyota owners is that they appreciate the convenience of mobile service. You don't have to drive your vehicle across town, sit in a waiting room, or arrange a ride back home. We come to your driveway, your office parking lot, or wherever your vehicle is parked in the Columbia or Lexington area.

Mobile detailing also allows us to work in a controlled environment. We bring our own water, power, and professional-grade equipment — so your detail isn't rushed or compromised by a shared shop bay. Every Toyota we detail gets our full attention for the duration of the service.

We service all of Columbia SC including Forest Acres, Irmo, and Harbison, as well as Lexington, Chapin, Cayce, and surrounding communities. If you're not sure whether you're within our service area, give us a call and we'll confirm.

Book Your Toyota Detail Today

Whether you're driving a commuter Corolla or a loaded Tundra Platinum, Mikah's Auto Detailing delivers professional results at fair, transparent prices. Check our full pricing page for the complete service menu, or call us directly to get a quote for your specific Toyota model and condition.

Call (803) 667-8731 to schedule your Toyota auto detailing service in Columbia or Lexington SC. Same-week appointments are usually available, and we're happy to provide a free quote before you commit.

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does it cost to detail a Toyota Camry in Columbia SC?

A Toyota Camry detail in Columbia SC starts at $225 for a Basic Detail (full exterior wash, interior vacuum, windows, and trim) and $400 for a Factory Reset full detail that includes clay bar decontamination, paint sealant, carpet and upholstery extraction, and leather conditioning. Pricing may vary based on the vehicle's current condition.

How much does it cost to detail a Toyota Tacoma?

Toyota Tacoma detailing in Columbia and Lexington SC ranges from $225–$250 for a Basic Detail to $400–$450 for a Factory Reset full detail. The bed is cleaned as part of our standard service; heavily soiled beds from work or off-road use may incur a small additional charge that we always communicate upfront.

Do you detail Toyota 4Runners and Tundras?

Yes. Toyota 4Runners and Sequoias start at $250 for a Basic Detail and $450 for a Factory Reset. Toyota Tundras are priced the same way, starting at $250 for a Basic Detail and $450+ for a Factory Reset depending on cab size and bed condition.

Is ceramic coating worth it for a Toyota in South Carolina?

Yes. South Carolina's UV intensity and humidity accelerate paint oxidation and clear coat degradation. Ceramic coating provides 2–5 years of UV-resistant, hydrophobic protection that significantly extends the life of Toyota factory paint and helps maintain resale value. We provide custom ceramic coating quotes — call (803) 667-8731 to discuss your vehicle.

Do you offer mobile Toyota detailing in Lexington SC?

Yes. We provide fully mobile Toyota auto detailing throughout Lexington SC and the surrounding area including Chapin, Cayce, and West Columbia. We bring all equipment and water to your location — no drop-off required. Call (803) 667-8731 to schedule.

Why does my Toyota have swirl marks?

Swirl marks on Toyota paint are most commonly caused by automated car washes with rotating brushes or cloth strips. They are also caused by improper hand wash technique using circular motions or dirty wash mitts. Paint correction can remove existing swirl marks, and ceramic coating after correction helps prevent new ones from forming.
